medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Over the past couple of years he has been experiencing flares of left epigastric pain. The pain is sharp, can radiate to the left flank, and is not associated with N/V/D. His most recent and severe episode was about 2 months ago when obstructing pancreatic calculi and a pseudocyst were discovered. He underwent 2 ERCP procedures but they were unsuccessful. Currently, his epigastric pain is managed by percocet q6hours.
medical_history.past_medical_history: PMH: CAD (MI x 2, stents x6), HTN, HLD. PSH: perforated diverticulitis s/p hemicolectomy and colostomy for one year ___ yrs ago; umbilical/incisional hernia repair x3; foot surgery.
medical_history.family_history: Positive for history of CVA, CAD, bone cancer, breast cancer, diverticulitis.

physical_examination.general_examination: Upon discharge: VS: 98.6, 90, 109/62, 14, 98% RA. ABD: Left subcostal incision open to air with steri strips and c/d/i. LLQ JP site with occlusive dressing and c/d/i. Old midline incision well healed.
